The only way for a soul tangled in sin to find relief is by turning to God alone. Many things seem like distractions, and many offer themselves as solutions, but the soul finds that the Lord alone can heal. And until people realize the guilt of their sin and completely surrender everything to come directly to God, it is useless for them to expect any relief. The Holy Spirit gives poor souls a fresh awareness of their deep need, prompting them to make earnest requests through prayer and by crying out to God. As they love their souls and are concerned for the glory of the Lord, they should not neglect this duty. Why do these matters remain uncertain for so long? Is it because of laziness and discouragement that they settle for ordinary and customary approaches to God? Then let us rise and get to work; it must be done, and it is safe to do so. We are to humble ourselves before God, acknowledging our guilt in his sight. Let us admit our sinfulness; we cannot justify ourselves or claim innocence. It is our greatest comfort that there is forgiveness with him, which is what we need. Jesus Christ is the great Ransom; he is always an Advocate for us, and through him we hope to obtain forgiveness. There is forgiveness with you, not so that you will feel entitled, but so that you may be reverent. The fear of God often represents the entire worship of God. The only reason and encouragement for sinners is this: there is forgiveness with the Lord.

My soul waits for the Lord, for his grace and the working of his power. We must hope for only what he has promised in his word. Like those who wish to see the dawn, eagerly wanting light to come long before day; even more earnestly a good person longs for signs of God’s favor and visits of his grace. Let all who dedicate themselves to the Lord cheerfully rely on him. This redemption is freedom from all sin. Jesus Christ saves his people from their sins, both from the condemning power and the commanding influence of sin. It is abundant redemption; there is a complete fullness in the Redeemer, enough for all, enough for each; therefore enough for me, says the believer. Redemption from sin includes redemption from all other evils, so it is an abundant redemption through the atoning blood of Jesus, who will redeem his people from all their sins. All who wait on God for mercy and grace are sure to have peace.
